metadata.greeting: This weekend is shaping up to be wonderful for families in Hackney! I've found lots of fun activities, both indoors and out, perfect for a 3-year-old. Plus, there are some exciting events coming up in the next few weeks that you can book ahead of time.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, E2 9PA
activities.description: The Young V&A is a vibrant and interactive museum designed specifically for children and families. It offers hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions to encourage imagination, play, and design.
activities.whyItWorks: The Mini Museum space is specifically designed for under 2s, offering a sensory explosion for babies and toddlers with lots to touch, hear and see.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check for special exhibitions like 'Making Egypt'.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: The Discover Children's Story Centre in Stratford offers two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: It hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for Baby Storytelling sessions.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: The Museum of London Docklands explores the history of London's waterfront with interactive exhibits and displays.
activities.whyItWorks: A Soft Play area is available.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the DLR to West India Quay.
transport.tip: Explore the old warehouses and see historic ships.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A hidden gem near Brick Lane with a community feel.
activities.whyItWorks: It provides a chance to see animals and experience a bit of countryside in the city.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then walk east.
transport.tip: Check out their line up of Easter activities.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Let your young one splash about in the fountains and playgrounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Space to run around and interactive water features are great for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Pack a change of clothes for the little one!

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: A puppet show based on the book by Julia Donaldson, about a little old lady who thinks her house is too small.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppetry is engaging for young children, and the story is simple and fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Angel, then walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as availability is low.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A community-focused farm in the heart of Hackney.
activities.whyItWorks: It offers a chance to see animals and learn about farming in an urban environment.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney.
transport.tip: Perfect for a quick visit and suitable for little legs.

activities.title: Geffrye Museum - Museum of the Home
activities.description: The museum explores homes, gardens and home life from 1600 to the present day.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for nostalgia such as showing the kids old phones, tvs and computers and how vacuum cleaners have evolved through time.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Hoxton.
transport.tip: There's also a fun trail for kids.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the wonders of the natural world with dinosaur exhibits and interactive displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaur-themed hunts and seasonal storytelling sessions cater to young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then the Circle or District line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book tickets online to skip the queues.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits, space exploration galleries, and more.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden is an interactive space for children to discover construction, water, light and sound through play. Pattern Pod is great for sensory fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then the Circle or District line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Consider booking tickets for Wonderlab for a hands-on experience.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: Meet farm animals and enjoy a bit of countryside in the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Farm animals are always a hit with toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria line to Vauxhall.
transport.tip: Check for Easter activities.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children, featuring an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: The enclosed space and variety of activities make it ideal for toddlers to let off steam.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Kings Cross.
transport.tip: Perfect for a summer visit when the paddling pool is open.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in the Hampstead Heath area that is completely free to enter and home to rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: It is home to wallabies, lemurs, Scottish wildcat and more.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath, then walk or take a short bus ride.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Greenwich Park Playground
activities.description: A large, traditional wooden playground with a maritime theme.
activities.whyItWorks: There is a great water area in the summer.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Greenwich.
transport.tip: The Cove playground is best for toddlers and older children.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: A museum with a variety of exhibits, gardens with views over London, farm animals, a music laboratory for children and a stunning conservatory.
activities.whyItWorks: Free workshops for children are offered daily.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Easy to spend a whole day in the museum and gardens.

activities.title: Easter at London Zoo
activities.description: Family fun, egg hunt and the joy of seeing the zoo's amazing animals this Easter holiday.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals are always a hit with toddlers and the egg hunt adds a festive element.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Tickets included in Zoo entry.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Enjoy a fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore a series of installations to find the bees, build them a new hive and help get them there safely.
activities.whyItWorks: An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond.
transport.tip: Tickets included in Kew Gardens entry.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail at Cutty Sark
activities.description: Follow the trail to spot hidden eggs around the ship. Collect the letters on each egg and unscramble the secret nautical code.
activities.whyItWorks: Exploring a historic ship and solving a puzzle is great fun for families.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Greenwich.
transport.tip: Check for family activities on Tuesday 31 March, Tuesday 7 April and Saturday 11 April 2026.

activities.title: We're Going On A Bear Hunt
activities.description: A musical adaptation of the classic children's book.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Wimbledon.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Family Easter Makes Workshop
activities.description: Family workshop making beautiful Easter nests.
activities.whyItWorks: Drop-in workshop is perfect for little ones.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath, then walk or take a short bus ride.
transport.tip: Drop in from 11am – 2pm.

activities.title: The Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Walk through the gardens, looking for elusive Lindt GOLD BUNNY statues peeking out from daffodils. There's chocolate prize at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Bunny hunt with a chocolate prize.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Included in palace admission.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: A heart-warming show aimed at 5-9s.
activities.whyItWorks: The Unicorn Theatre is dedicated to children's theatre.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to London Bridge then walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: British Library – Fairy Tales
activities.description: Explore enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, brought to life through books, theatre costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: There are interactive elements throughout – including a magic mirror, a genie and even magical potions – making it more than just a display to look at.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Euston.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.
